news.title: Close encounters: England have to crack Argentina’s ability to prevent close-range shots
news.lede: Argentina’s underlying defensive process has been stronger than the outcomes so England must make potent use of their set pieces. Argentina’s defence has not always looked befitting of World Cup champions this summer. Cape Verde, Egypt and Jordan have collectively scored five goals against them, with their only clean sheets coming against Algeria and Austria. The ease with which relative minnows have broken the backline of Lionel Scaloni’s side should offer ample encouragement to Jude Bellingham and Harry Kane.

news.title: World Cup final half-time to last up to 25 minutes
news.lede: The half-time show for last year's Club World Cup final took place on a stage constructed in the top tier of the New York New Jersey Stadium. The half-time break in Sunday's World Cup final is expected to last between 20 and 25 minutes. An 11-minute Super Bowl-style show, co-headlined by Madonna, Shakira and K-pop boy band BTS, will take place during the interval at New York New Jersey Stadium. While sources within World Cup organisers Fifa suggest the break will be around 20 minutes, it is understood that one option is for the normal 15-minute half-time break and then the planned 11-minute show.

news.title: World Cup goal leaders: Kylian Mbappé leads Lionel Messi in Golden Boot race, with pair of English stars in the mix
news.lede: The stars have been out throughout the 2026 World Cup. There are a handful of them among the final four teams. We've been tracking the race for the Golden Boot, the award that's given to the World Cup's top goal scorer. Assists are used as a tiebreaker if two or more players have the same goal total.

news.title: Lionel Messi’s first meeting with England will be a contest of will and aura
news.lede: The Messi arc reaches a decisive point in Atlanta – passage to a third final or an exit to test the limits of Argentina’s love for their unassuming athlete-genius. Wednesday night, Atlanta Stadium, 101 games down, three left to play, and finally it makes sense. Bring on The Countdown, that moment just before kick-off in every one of those quietly fascinating World Cup matches where suddenly the world’s most excited man is bellowing over the PA system in a state of outraged, crowing transport, like the last voice you’ll ever hear before the American century explodes in a ball of inanity, fried chicken and porn. …” the world’s most excited man shouts, prelude to some cautious rolling possession, maybe an early back-pass, and an agreeable reminder that the game itself will not be stage managed.

news.title: Football Extra: Readers' World Cup memorabilia
news.lede: Meet 60 year old World Cup Willy. In 1966 I lived near Wembley Stadium and went to many games there including the final. He's definitely showing his age (as am I!), but I'm hoping he'll continue to bring England luck from his current home in Wilmington NC, USA. my programmes and some of the tickets from WC '66.

news.title: Spain stun France to reach World Cup 2026 final
news.lede: Spain stunned tournament favorites France 2-0 to reach the World Cup final on Tuesday, July 14, producing a tactical masterpiece to shatter Les Bleus' dreams of winning the title for the third time. A first-half penalty from Mikel Oyarzabal and a 58th-minute Pedro Porro goal sealed an utterly clinical victory for the European champions at the AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas. Spain, World Cup winners in 2010, will now face either Argentina or England in Sunday's final in New Jersey. "We started almost four years ago with an idea and we've been faithful to that idea and it's brought us here," said Spain coach Luis de la Fuente, hailing his team.

news.title: Deschamps' golden France era to end after another heartbreak, but legacy intact
Is it Spain’s tournament to lose? Re-ranking the World Cup teams with three games left
news.lede: Spain are through to the final after beating France on Tuesday.
